About: It is about the London Underground.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with The Jam song "Going Underground".
ConsistsOf: It consists of parody comic songs.
CreatedBy: It was created by Adam Kay and Suman Biswas.
HasA: It has a song about the London Underground.
It has a song parodying the Jam song "Going Underground".
It has a song called "London Underground".
It has a song called "Anaesthetist's Hymn".
It has a song called "Careless Surgeon".
It has a song called "Always Look on the Bright Side of Life".
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by parody comic songs.
It is characterized by successful years at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe.
It is characterized by a popular internet fad in the UK in 2005.
It is characterized by a flash animation.
It is characterized by criticism of the London Underground strike of the time.
HeldIn: It was held in Edinburgh Festival Fringe.
It was held in Trondheim, Norway.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by The Jam song "Going Underground".
IsA: It is a parody music band.
It is a band fronted by Adam Kay and Suman Biswas.
It is a live recorded album.
KnownFor: It is known for a song about the London Underground.
It is known for parody comic songs mostly dealing with medical subjects.
It is known for its parodies based on the works of American comedic songwriter Tom Lehrer.
It is known for its successful years at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe.
It is known for its song "London Underground".
It is known for its accompanying flash animation.
It is known for its criticism of the London Underground strike of the time.
It is known for its album "Fitness to Practice".
It is known for its album "Unfit to Practise".
It is known for its album "Have Yourself a Sweary Little Christmas".
MemberOf: It is a member of Macmillan Cancer Relief.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe.
It occurred at the iTunes Store.
PartOf: It is part of Geek Pop 2008.
PerformedBy: It was performed by Adam Kay.
It was performed by Suman Biswas.
PublishedAs: It was published as a single on the iTunes Store.
PublishedIn: It was published in the iTunes Store.
UsedFor: It is used for parody comic songs.
It is used for healing the sick and lame.
UsedIn: It is used in the NHS.
UsedTo: It is used to ridicule those who study medicine at rival London medical schools.
WrittenBy: It was written by Adam Kay.
